children.text: A keyword is an ability which conveys specific common rules. Each keyword has its own rules which can be found on the keyword list below. These are all
and follow all of the rules and restrictions of named abilities.

Abilities do not usually stack (for example, if you have the Dead-ly keyword on your weapon and gain it from another source, you cannot use both of them to roll two additional Crit dice). There are, however, some abilities that do stack: unless the ability text says otherwise, every keyword followed by a number is a

.You may activate several stackable keyword abilities with the same name at the same time. Their effects then add up and “stack.” For example, two separate Opening 1 abilities would effectively be the same as Opening 2.Keywords that are not followed by a number
The following list contains keywords common to all Cycles of
. Keywords that are introduced in a specific Cy-cle will be described in that Cycle’s Storybook. All of these are ref-erenced in the Master Index (see p. 76).
Your Ambrosia Limit becomes X or is in-creased by +X.
Ambrosia Limit X does not stack, but all +X bonuses are cumulative.
This  ignores Power Level penalties (irrelevant in Cy-cle I).
During an Attack Roll, you may re-roll up to X Attack dice with no additional cost. Remember that you can only re-roll each die once, regardless of the re-roll source.
During the first ability window, place X Break tokens in the Kratos Pool (see Break X keyword).
While performing an Evasion Roll, treat X successful hits as evaded.
During the second ability window, place X Break to-kens in the Kratos Pool. During the Power Roll step, each Break token in the Kratos Pool allows the attacking Titan to turn 1  symbol into 1 .
You may move through other Titans. You cannot end your voluntary movement on a space occupied by another Titan.
When you wound the Primordial, test Wisdom (8+). On a success, you immediately gain X additional non-Core Primordial resources from that BP card. Note: These resources are
To attack with this weapon, gain +1.

The Titan subjected to Crash gains +1  and suffers Knock-down. A Titan suffers Crash each time a Primordial moves onto or through its space, as well as when any effect forces it to move onto a space with an Obstacle Terrain tile or places an Obstacle Terrain tile on its.
During the second ability window, if you successfully wounded with this weapon, exhaust it. Treat it as a voluntary exhaust.
During an Attack Roll, replace one of your regular Attack dice with an additional Crit die. If you roll only one Attack die, you gain a Crit Chance on a natural 9–10 result instead.
Move the displaced target to an empty space adjacent to its current position. If a Titan is displaced and there is no space it can be legally displaced to, it dies instead.

During the second ability window, place a Diver-sion token in the Kratos Pool. An attacking Titan may use a Diversion token from the Kratos Pool to ignore Fail Responses until the end of their Attack.
When you are about to resolve an Evasion Roll, gain +1

During the second ability window, you may discard X Despair tokens or Ambrosia tokens to place X Opening to-kens in the Kratos Pool (see Opening X keyword).
BP cards (remove each one from the BP deck and shuffle in a card one level higher), one at a time. Then do the same for the AI deck. Do not look at the removed cards.
If your Titan is dead, at the End of Battle roll a d10. On a ‘1’, your Argonaut dies as well.
Take the top card from the Wound Stack and remove it from the Battle. It does not count as a Wound and does not provide re-sources at the end of the Battle.
During the Draw BP card step of the Attack, you may look at the top two BP cards and choose which one to attack. Shuffle the other card back into the deck.
